# context/ Files Standard — Dynamic Data and Autocomplete Chain

**Date:** 2026-04-01
**Status:** ACTIVE STANDARD
**Type:** Agent file standard (outer layer, refreshed every 30s by brain)
**Source:** fleet-elevation/02 (architecture), fleet-elevation/04 (brain, autocomplete chain)
**Injection:** fleet-context.md at position 6, task-context.md at position 7
**Generated by:** fleet/core/preembed.py, fleet/core/context_assembly.py
**NOT hand-written.** These files are GENERATED by the orchestrator every cycle.

---

## 1. Purpose

context/ files are the DYNAMIC DATA layer — refreshed every 30 seconds
by the orchestrator. They contain current fleet state, task details,
and the engineered autocomplete chain. The agent reads these to know
what's happening NOW and what to do about it.

These files are the OUTER ring of the onion. They reference rules
(CLAUDE.md), identity (IDENTITY.md), and tools (TOOLS.md) — but those
files don't reference context/. Dependencies point INWARD.

**context/ files are NEVER compressed or summarized.** Full data always.
This is a PO requirement:

> "we went from 7% to 0% and you are still alive"
> The agent needs FULL data to work effectively.

---

## 2. fleet-context.md — Heartbeat Pre-Embedded Data

### Required Sections (in order)

| # | Section | Content | Source Module |
|---|---------|---------|--------------|
| 1 | `## Fleet State` | work_mode, cycle_phase, backend_mode, agents online count | fleet_mode.py |
| 2 | `## PO Directives` | Active directives targeting this agent or "all" | orchestrator.py (directive parser) |
| 3 | `## Messages` | @mentions since last heartbeat (full content, not count) | board_memory filtered by mention:{agent} |
| 4 | `## Assigned Tasks` | FULL task details for all assigned tasks (verbatim, stage, readiness, artifacts, comments) | preembed.py format_task_full() |
| 5 | `## Role Data` | Role-specific data from role_providers | role_providers.py |
| 6 | `## Events` | Relevant events since last heartbeat | event_chain.py filtered |
| 7 | `## Context Awareness` | Context usage %, rate limit usage % (informational) | session_telemetry.py |

### Per-Role Data (Section 5)

| Role | Role Data Includes | Source |
|------|-------------------|--------|
| PM | Unassigned tasks (full detail), sprint metrics (done/total/blocked/velocity), Plane sprint data, pending gates | project_manager_provider |
| Fleet-Ops | Pending approvals (full context), review queue (time in review), health alerts, budget status, agent statuses | fleet_ops_provider |
| Architect | Tasks needing design input, high-complexity flags, architecture decisions in board memory | architect_provider |
| DevSecOps | PRs needing security review (full diff context), security alerts, infrastructure health | devsecops_provider (to build) |
| Workers | In-progress artifact state (completeness %, what's done, what's missing), suggested readiness | worker_provider |

### Validation Criteria

- [ ] NEVER compressed or summarized — FULL data
- [ ] All 7 sections present in order
- [ ] Assigned tasks include verbatim requirement (when task has one)
- [ ] Role data matches role_providers.py output for this role
- [ ] Context awareness includes BOTH context % and rate limit %
- [ ] Events are filtered to relevance (not all fleet events)
- [ ] PO directives appear FIRST after fleet state

---

## 3. task-context.md — Autocomplete Chain

### Required Sections (in order — ORDER IS CRITICAL)

The autocomplete chain is ENGINEERED so the AI's natural continuation
is the correct action. Each section narrows the response space.

| # | Section | Content | Why This Order |
|---|---------|---------|---------------|
| 1 | `# YOU ARE: {name} ({role})` | Identity grounding | AI generates from this identity |
| 2 | `# YOUR TASK: {title}` | Task focus | Narrows to specific work |
| 3 | `# YOUR STAGE: {stage}` | Stage awareness | Constrains to stage-appropriate actions |
| 4 | `# READINESS: {readiness}%` | Progress | Shows how close to work stage |
| 5 | `## VERBATIM REQUIREMENT` | PO's exact words — NEVER summarized | The anchor. Everything references this. |
| 6 | `## STAGE PROTOCOL` | What to do in this stage (MUST/MUST NOT/CAN) | Stage-gated instructions from stage_context.py |
| 7 | `## INPUTS FROM COLLEAGUES` | Contributions received (design, tests, security, UX) | Requirements from synergy |
| 8 | `## DELIVERY PHASE: {phase}` | Phase standards that apply | Quality bar for this work |
| 9 | `## WHAT TO DO NOW` | Action directive | The prompt the AI completes |
| 10 | `## WHAT HAPPENS WHEN YOU ACT` | Chain documentation for likely next tool call | Confidence in consequences |

### Section Details

**Section 5 — Verbatim Requirement:**
```markdown
## VERBATIM REQUIREMENT
> {PO's exact words, unmodified, unsummarized}
```
This is the ANCHOR. The agent references this throughout their work.
It NEVER changes. It's the source of truth for what was asked.

**Section 6 — Stage Protocol:**
Generated by stage_context.py. Example for analysis stage:
```markdown
## STAGE PROTOCOL (analysis)
MUST: Read relevant code. Produce analysis_document artifact progressively.
MUST NOT: Produce solutions. Write code. Call fleet_commit.
CAN PRODUCE: analysis_document (findings, implications, scope, current_state)
HOW TO ADVANCE: Complete analysis → readiness increases → PM reviews
```

**Section 7 — Colleague Contributions:**
```markdown
## INPUTS FROM COLLEAGUES
### Architect (design_input)
Approach: Observer pattern in fleet/core/events.py
Target files: event_chain.py, chain_handlers.py
Constraints: Dependencies point inward. No direct MC imports in handlers.

### QA (qa_test_definition)
TC-001: Event dispatch invokes all registered handlers (unit, required)
TC-002: Cascade depth limit prevents infinite loops (unit, required)
TC-003: Partial failure doesn't break chain (integration, required)
```

**Section 9 — Action Directive:**
Generated by brain based on stage, readiness, and role:
```markdown
## WHAT TO DO NOW
You are in ANALYSIS stage. Read the codebase for {scope area}.
Build your analysis_document progressively. Each finding:
fleet_artifact_update("findings", append=True, value={finding}).
When analysis complete, post summary as task comment.
```

### Validation Criteria

- [ ] All 10 sections present in EXACT order (order IS the autocomplete chain)
- [ ] Verbatim requirement present and UNMODIFIED (if task has one)
- [ ] Stage protocol matches stage_context.py for current stage
- [ ] Contributions included when received (not omitted)
- [ ] Phase standards present when task has delivery_phase
- [ ] Action directive is stage-appropriate and role-appropriate
- [ ] Chain documentation matches the likely next tool call
- [ ] NEVER compressed or summarized — full detail throughout

---

## 4. IaC Flow

context/ files are NOT provisioned from templates — they are GENERATED
by the orchestrator at runtime.

```
fleet/core/preembed.py              # builds heartbeat pre-embed
fleet/core/context_assembly.py      # assembles task context
fleet/core/stage_context.py         # stage instructions
fleet/core/role_providers.py        # per-role data
fleet/core/autocomplete.py          # autocomplete chain builder (to build)
     ↓
orchestrator Step 0: context refresh  # writes files every 30s
     ↓
agents/{name}/context/
  ├── fleet-context.md               # heartbeat data
  └── task-context.md                # task autocomplete chain
     ↓
gateway reads on heartbeat           # injected at positions 6, 7
```

**The standard for context/ files is enforced in CODE, not in templates.**
preembed.py and context_assembly.py must produce output matching this
standard. Unit tests validate structure.

---

## 5. Relationship to Other Standards

| Standard Doc | Relationship |
|-------------|-------------|
| claude-md-standard.md | CLAUDE.md has RULES. context/ has DATA the rules act on. |
| heartbeat-md-standard.md | HEARTBEAT.md has PROTOCOL. context/ has STATE the protocol responds to. |
| identity-soul-standard.md | IDENTITY/SOUL are CONSTANT. context/ is DYNAMIC (refreshed every cycle). |
| tools-agents-standard.md | TOOLS.md has tool DOCS. context/ has WHEN to use them (action directive). |
| brain-modules-standard.md | Brain GENERATES context/. Standard is enforced in brain code. |
